mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the prior three months reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following 3-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that triggers de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month changes in payroll employment and retail sales signal robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months where both PAYEMS and RSAFS post positive month-over-month changes do not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month contraction in industrial production predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns because supply-side weakness confirms slowing growth and pressures corporate margins.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%, month-over-month INDPRO contractions do not coincide with negative 5-day returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative decline in housing starts exceeding one hundred thousand units signals collapsing residential investment and forward demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous three-month rise in the federal funds rate and a three-month rise in the unemployment rate define a stagflationary monetary tightening that compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint rises in FEDFUNDS and UNRATE coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in retail sales signals resilient consumer demand that supports near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes do not correlate with positive 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP signal an official recession that destroys corporate revenues and triggers sustained equity selling, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Recession signals from consecutive GDPC1 declines coincide with positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances.
